title: quercus × hastingsii
intro: Quercus × hastingsii, also known as the Hastings oak, is a hybrid species of oak tree that combines characteristics of Quercus agrifolia and Quercus lobata. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Quercus × hastingsii include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
